Details: Biological degradation of nitrobenzene using a sequential anaerobic-aerobic process (54 to 100 percent), Shewanella putrefaciens CN32 (53 to 100 percent), bacteria containing toluene degradative pathways (50 to 100 percent), and Comamonas sp. Strain JS765 (28 to 100 percent) was found to be highly effective in bench-scale studies.  Biodegradation can be used to achieve higher nitrobenzene degradation.
